notes:
BLOCKED — REQUIRES EVM WALLET SIGNING (not achievable with the current HTTP-only primitives).

Moltmail (moltmail.io, by EtherMail) cannot be provisioned via HTTP-only calls. The auth flow requires EVM wallet signing:
  1. POST /nonce → returns a nonce integer
  2. Agent must produce a secp256k1 signature over 'By signing this message you agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy\n\nNONCE: {nonce}'
  3. POST /authenticate with the signature → returns a JWT
  4. First login: POST /users/onboarding with {email:'', isSso:false} + JWT → account created
  5. Email is deterministic: {walletAddress.toLowerCase()}@moltmail.io

There is no API-key or password-based registration path. The wallet can be generated locally, but signing step 2 needs a crypto library (ethers.js/viem) — not curl-reachable. Once a JWT exists, all inbox operations are standard HTTPS to srv.ethermail.io.

Probes confirmed 2026-04-20 by Sonnet reviewer:
  POST /nonce                      → 200 {success:true, nonce:1}
  POST /authenticate (bad sig)      → 403 {error:'Invalid signature length'}
  POST /users/onboarding (no token) → 403 {error:'Invalid accessToken value'}

Source: https://github.com/EtherMailOrg/moltmail-openclaw

UNBLOCKING PATH: Add ethers.js (or a web3.py equivalent) to the agent sandbox, generate an ephemeral wallet, perform signing steps 2-4 programmatically. No human approval, CAPTCHA, or phone verification required — it's a pure crypto-library dependency. Same shape as ClawTasks which already relies on `npm install ethers` inside the Daytona sandbox.

Until the unblocking work lands, the tests below intentionally fail at the signup step so the verifier records an honest 'blocked' result rather than silently passing.

steps.description: POST /nonce — confirms the endpoint is live. Signup cannot proceed further without secp256k1 signing, which the current primitives do not support.
POST /authenticate with a known-bad signature. Expected to 403 — which is the 'blocked' signal. When secp256k1 signing lands, this step gets replaced with a real signed payload.

assertions.description: INTENTIONALLY FAILS until EVM signing is wired. See notes.
INTENTIONALLY FAILS — no JWT is produced without a valid signature.
